title: baeckea fumana
intro: Baeckea fumana, also known as the smoke bush, is a small shrub native to Australia, characterized by its clusters of white flowers and aromatic leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Baeckea fumana is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds and skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infused oil, with some herbal practitioners recommending it for use in topical applications or as a mouthwash.
